arts.ttl: Real Madrid-linked Rodri delivers update on Manchester City future
arts.sum: Manchester City midfielder Rodri delivers an update on his future at the Etihad Stadium amid reported interest from Real Madrid.

arts.ttl: Man City want Real Madrid target to sign new contract 'as soon as possible'
arts.sum: Manchester City are hoping to secure the long-term future of integral midfielder Rodri "as soon as possible", despite the Euro 2024 winner still having almost three years left on his contract. The timing is considered "standard practice" for City, but the Premier League champions are described as having "always suspected" that Madrid would eventually show interest.

arts.ttl: Seedorf: “Real Madrid are favourites, but Inter, Milan and Juve can compete with the best”
arts.sum: The Champions League legend Clarence Seedorf shared his thoughts on the new format and top contenders for this season’s competition in an interview with La Gazzetta dello Sport. “I’m curious about the new format. I liked the previous one, as the anticipation for big matches was part of the spectacle. But let’s give the new format time to show how it works,” Seedorf said. When asked about the favorites, he stated: “History tells us that Real Madrid is the team to beat, but many can compete. The gap has narrowed and there are no easy matches – that’s the beauty The post Seedorf: “Real Madrid are favourites, but Inter, Milan and Juve can compete with the best” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Carlo Ancelotti reveals what is to blame for Real Madrid injury crisis
arts.sum: Real Madrid coach Carlo Ancelotti has blamed the "too demanding" football calendar for the club's growing injury problems, calling for the number of games to be reduced. "We've looked at what we can do, but it's out of our hands," he suggested this week as Madrid prepare to begin the new eight-game Champions League 'league phase'.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid 'plotting' ambitious triple transfer raid on Premier League
arts.sum: Real Madrid are reportedly eyeing up the capture of three Premier League stars ahead of the upcoming transfer windows in January and the summer of 2025.

arts.ttl: “Nothing more to say” – Man City ace conveniently dodges questions about reported Real Madrid interest
arts.sum: Man City begin their 2024/25 Champions League campaign with a classic fixture against the team they overcame in the 2023 final to win the treble; Inter Milan. It is one of a host of first night fixtures that sees two of European football’s biggest sides going head to head. arts.ttl: Real Madrid plan SENSATIONAL transfer raid of top Premier League stars
arts.sum: According to the Independent, Real will target moves for Trent Alexander-Arnold, with his contract at Liverpool set to expire next summer, as well as Manchester City defensive midfielder Rodri . Alexander-Arnold to headline Real transfer plans
